data.title: 2 'Perfect 10' Stocks Analysts Think Are Strong Buys
data.description:
Every investor aims to find the perfect stock, but locating the shares that check every box is no easy task. The stock market throws up a massive wall of information. Thousands of traders are dealing in thousands of companies, generating millions of transactions each day. The result is an imposing mountain of data that can make separating signal from noise a real challenge.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fortunately, investors don't have to tackle that mountain of information alone. TipRanks' Smart Score harnesses AI and natural language processing to sift through the market's vast data universe, organizing and interpreting it in a way that helps investors make smarter decisions.

The system analyzes each stock using a set of proven factors that historically correlate with future outperformance. These signals are then distilled into a single, easy-to-read rating on a scale from 1 to 10. A "Perfect 10" suggests that multiple data-driven indicators are lining up in the stock's favor – a strong signal that the name may deserve a closer look. In this way, the Smart Score turns stock selection into something closer to a science than a guessing game.

With that in mind, we've opened up the TipRanks database to look for stocks that carry that Perfect 10 score – and not only that, they have also earned 'Strong Buy' support from Wall Street's analysts. Here are two stocks that stand out.

JBS (JBS)

We don't think about it as often as it deserves, but food is big business. The first Perfect 10 stock on our radar today, JBS, is a $16 billion player in the global food industry, and a world-leading supplier in several areas. The company boasts that it is the #1 global producer for both poultry and beef, and the #2 global producer for pork. On a regional level, JBS is the #1 egg producer in South America, the #2 salmon producer in Australia, and the #3 plant-based foods producer in Europe.

JBS was founded in Brazil in 1953, and today operates headquarters in both Sao Paulo and Amsterdam. The company employs over 280,000 people globally, operates more than 250 production facilities worldwide, and has sales relationships with customers in over 180 countries.

The food industry can be a major source of pollution in the world's environments, and JBS makes a point of addressing these issues at the source. The company has a commitment to promoting sustainable agriculture and supply chains, starting with stewarding natural resources at farms, and then sourcing responsible commodities. This is followed by setting up sustainable food production chains – all to allow for stronger food supply systems. The goal is to reduce the environmental impact while still feeding the global population.

JBS last reported earnings for 3Q25; its next report, covering 4Q25, is due out later this month. The company's quarterly net sales in Q3 came to $22.6 billion, for a 13% year-over-year increase. JBS's bottom line, the EPS of 52 cents, was down 16% from 3Q24, although it beat the forecast by a nickel.

UBS analyst Matheus Enfeldt is upbeat about JBS and sees it as a potential winner going forward. He says, "We expect JBS to appreciate as it continues to rerate following the US listing and joins indices in the country, a process which typically lasts for 2+ years. The cherry on top of the cake is that we believe returns from company's ~US$1bn annual expansion capex remain unpriced by the market, which may add ~1% upside in FCFEy per year vs what is priced in, and given JBS's solid growth track record we see some upside is warranted. We acknowledge buying into cycle weakness may be challenging, however the micro side of the story is sufficiently strong to deliver stock performance in our view."

Enfeldt follows up these comments with a Buy rating for the stock and a $19.50 price target that suggests a share price gain of 28% in the coming months.

The Strong Buy consensus rating here is unanimous, based on 5 recent positive analyst reviews. JBS is trading at a price of $15.21, and its $20.75 average price target indicates room for a gain of 36% on the one-year horizon. (See JBS stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: 'Taking to the Skies': Joby Aviation's (JOBY) First Air Taxi Begins Test Flights
data.description:
Electric air taxis have long sounded like something from the future. Joby Aviation (JOBY) is trying to bring that future closer. The company said it has begun flying its first production aircraft, an important step as it works toward launching passenger service.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The early flights are taking place at Joby's facility in Marina, California, where company pilots are testing the aircraft before federal regulators step in. These flights will help prepare the aircraft for certification checks by the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) later this year.

Joby has already spent years working with regulators to approve the aircraft's design, parts, and production plans. Its development aircraft have also logged more than 50,000 miles in earlier test flights.

A New Kind of Aircraft

Joby's air taxi is designed to work like a mix between a helicopter and a plane. It uses six rotors to lift off and land vertically, then flies forward like a traditional aircraft.

The electric aircraft can carry one pilot and four passengers, and the company says it is designed to be quieter than helicopters while producing no direct emissions.

First Passenger Flights Could Come Soon

Joby hopes to launch its first passenger service in Dubai later this year. Two of the planned four landing sites for the service are already being built in the city.

The company is also preparing for limited operations in the United States as part of a federal program aimed at bringing electric air taxis into the national airspace.

Looking further ahead, Joby plans to increase production at its facilities in California and Dayton, Ohio, with a target of building four aircraft per month by 2027.

Is Joby Aviation Stock a Good Buy? 

On TipRanks, JOBY stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with two Buys, four Holds and two Sells assigned in the last three months. The average JOBY stock price target is $13.25, suggesting a potential upside of 29.52% from the current level.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Casino Giant Caesars Stock (CZR) Surges as Billionaires Launch Takeover Battle
data.description:
Shares in Caesars Entertainment (CZR), which operates the largest number of casino properties in the U.S., jumped about 12% on Wednesday. This followed a report by the Wall Street Journal that billionaire Tilman Fertitta made a roughly $7 billion counteroffer for the resort chain operator.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fertitta Entertainment Makes $7 Billion Buyout Offer

The offer from Fertitta's firm, Fertitta Entertainment, breaks down to about $34 per share, topping an all-cash offer of roughly $33 per share from billionaire Carl Icahn's Icahn Enterprises (IEP), the outlet said, citing insider sources. The company is reported not to have made a decision yet on the lower offer.

Caesars operates dozens of resort brands, including the flagship luxury and high-end dining brand Caesars Palace. It also owns brands such as Harrah's, Horseshoe, Eldorado, and Tropicana.

Fertitta's bid would fold Caesars into Fertitta Entertainment's broader empire, which includes the Golden Nugget casino chain, the Landry's dining‑hospitality‑gaming group, and the basketball club Houston Rockets. On the other hand, Icahn Enterprises operates as a diversified conglomerate with investments in energy, real estate, automotive, and food packaging, among others.

Caesars Confronts Softer Leisure Demand, Prediction-Market Jitters

The offer comes as Caesars continues to show consistent revenue growth, but operating profit from same-store sales remains a challenge due to lower occupancy of its properties in Las Vegas — the result of softer leisure travel bookings. Moreover, CZR has only gained about 6% over the last 12 months, as the rise of popular online prediction marketplaces such as Polymarket has spooked investors.

Meanwhile, shares of Vici Properties (VICI), which owns much of the real estate underpinning Caesars' operations, fell on the news. The real estate investment trust was created in 2017 as part of the bankruptcy reorganization of Caesars' operating unit. 

Some investors see Vici as a likely complication for potential buyout efforts, especially regarding plans to carve out Caesars' digital gaming operations. However, the new proposals are being tailored to not require the trust's consent, the outlet reported.

Is CZR a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, analysts approach Caesars Entertainment's shares with caution and have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on the stock. This is based on eight Buys and six Holds issued over the past three months.

Moreover, the average CZR price target of $30.36 implies just about 4% upside.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Oil Is Surging Again after Tanker Attacks — 5 Energy Stocks to Watch Now
data.description:
Oil prices are surging again as the conflict in the Middle East threatens one of the world's most important energy routes. Fresh strikes on ships near the Strait of Hormuz have rattled markets and sparked fears of a major supply shock.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Crude has reacted quickly. At the time of writing, the price of the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) has pushed past $100 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate has also jumped to $94.5 per barrel after gaining more than 8% in recent trading. The rally comes as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz slows following reports of new attacks on commercial vessels.

The stakes are high. The Strait of Hormuz is the most critical oil shipping route in the world, carrying roughly 20% of global crude supply. Any disruption there can tighten supply almost instantly and send prices sharply higher. With markets now pricing in the risk of a prolonged disruption, oil traders are once again bracing for a potential energy shock.

Emergency Oil Release Fails to Ease Supply Fears

Governments have also tried to calm markets. On March 11, the International Energy Agency said its 32 member countries will release 400 million barrels from emergency reserves, including 172 million barrels from the United States.

However, analysts warned that this is a temporary measure. The reserves cannot fully replace the large volume of oil that normally moves through the Strait of Hormuz each day if the disruption continues.

5 Energy Stocks Investors Are Watching as Oil Surges

With oil prices suddenly back above $100, attention is shifting to the companies that produce it. When crude spikes, large oil producers and drilling companies often move higher as the value of their output rises.

  • Exxon Mobil (XOM): Exxon Mobil, the largest publicly traded U.S. oil producer, is often one of the first stocks investors watch when crude prices jump. The company operates across the full energy chain, from oil production to refining and chemicals, which allows it to capture more profit when energy prices rise. On Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on XOM, with analysts projecting about 49% upside.
  • Chevron (CVX): A major global producer with significant assets in the U.S. and around the world. Chevron's stock is highly sensitive to crude price movements, often serving as a primary vehicle for investors seeking direct exposure to oil rallies. According to analysts, CVX has a Moderate Buy rating and roughly 1.02% downside.
  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY): Known for its massive footprint in the Permian Basin, one of the most productive shale regions in the U.S. Occidental is well-positioned to see immediate cash flow benefits when oil prices are elevated. At $52, OXY's average price target suggests about 6.44% downside from current levels.
  • SLB (SLB): This is a leading oilfield services provider. Instead of producing oil itself, SLB provides the technology and expertise others need to extract it. When oil prices are high, producers often ramp up exploration and drilling, which drives demand for SLB's specialized services. On Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on SLB, with analysts projecting about 12.7% upside.
  • EOG Resources (EOG): A top-tier U.S. shale operator recognized for its operational efficiency. EOG's profitability is tightly correlated with market prices for crude, making it a key stock to watch during periods of high oil volatility. At $135.35, EOG's average price target suggests about 2.14% upside from current levels.

Which Is the Best Oil Stock to Buy?

TipRanks Best Oil Stocks tool shows oil field services giant Schlumberger currently makes for the best investment.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which energy stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'Buy' Occidental and Murphy Oil: Piper Sandler Bangs the Drum on OXY and MUR Stocks amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description:
Oil prices have climbed in recent days as tensions in the Middle East raise concerns about supply disruptions. With crude prices climbing, top Piper Sandler analyst Mark Lear turned more bullish on two energy producers — Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and Murphy Oil Corporation (MUR) — upgrading both stocks to Overweight and raising their price targets.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The analyst believes both companies offer strong leverage to higher oil prices and could benefit if crude remains elevated.

Why Piper Sandler Turned Bullish on Occidental (OXY)

Lear upgraded Occidental to Overweight from Neutral and lifted his price target to $66 from $54, indicating 19% upside from current levels. The analyst said Occidental remains one of the strongest operators in the Delaware Basin, a key U.S. oil region. Even though operating performance softened slightly in 2025, the company continues to deliver solid production results.

The analyst pointed to the company's 2026 outlook, which shows stronger capital efficiency. Occidental now expects to spend about $800 million less in capital while producing roughly the same level of oil and gas. While some investors questioned whether the savings could last, the company said the gains are sustainable, and Piper Sandler does not see this as a near-term risk for the stock.

Looking ahead, Lear believes the stock has strong upside if oil prices continue to rise, since Occidental's earnings are highly tied to crude prices.

Piper Sandler Sees 21% Upside in Murphy Oil (MUR)

Lear also upgraded Murphy Oil to Overweight from Neutral and raised his price target to $41 from $33, suggesting 21% upside from current levels. The analyst had previously downgraded the stock after the company's third-quarter 2025 results, when investors were closely watching exploration drilling in Cote d'Ivoire. The results from that project disappointed, and the company's 2026 guidance also came in below expectations, which weighed on the stock.

Despite that setback, Lear believes Murphy has strong operating leverage. This means the company's earnings could rise faster if oil prices move higher.

Looking ahead, Lear pointed to a potential catalyst in Vietnam, where Murphy is developing the Hai Su Vang (HSV) project. Further appraisal work expected in the first half of 2026 could help investors better understand the size of the resource and may support the stock.

What Wall Street Says About OXY and Murphy Oil

Turning to Wall Street, Murphy Oil and Occidental both currently carry a Hold consensus rating from analysts. Murphy Oil has an average price target of $30.50, which suggests about 10% downside from current levels.

Meanwhile, Occidental's average price target is $52.00, implying roughly 6% downside from its current price. This suggests OXY stock has slightly less downside risk compared with Murphy Oil based on current analyst estimates.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/12/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall as Rising Oil Prices Weigh on Investor Sentiment
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday as rising oil prices amid the escalating Iran conflict impacted investor sentiment. Energy Secretary Chris Wright's announcement that the U.S. will release 172 million barrels of oil from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and the International Energy Agency's (IEA) decision to release 400 million barrels from emergency reserves failed to bring down oil prices as Iran continues to disrupt supply. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.49%, 0.50%, and 0.59%, respectively, at 5:15 a.m. EST on March 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the price of the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up about 7% to $98.08 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) rose more than 6% to trade at $92.50 per barrel. Iran continues to strike foreign ships in the Persian Gulf and has warned that oil prices could climb to $200 a barrel.  

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed mixed as traders digested developments in the U.S.-Iran war. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones fell 0.08% and 0.61%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.08%.

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Adobe (ADBE), Dollar General (DG), and Ulta Beauty (ULTA). Coming to Thursday's economic releases, traders await updates on housing starts and jobless claims to gain further insights into the state of the U.S. economy.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.23%. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price was around $5,176 per ounce, as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indexes trended lower as investors assessed the updates on the U.S.-Iran conflict and its impact on oil prices.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Thursday

Asia-Pacific markets closed lower on March 12 as investors digested oil price volatility and escalating tensions in the Middle East.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index declined 0.70%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.10%, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.63%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei closed lower by 1.04%, with the Topix fell 1.32%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Faces EV Headwinds as Software Bets Rise
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock faces growing headwinds in its electric vehicle (EV) business even as the company ramps up its bets on software, artificial intelligence (AI), and energy platforms. Interestingly, the rationale is not tied to whether EV demand can accelerate. Instead, the debate centers on whether Wall Street views Tesla as merely a high-cost automaker, despite how its long-term trajectory angles toward software, AI, and energy infrastructure. 

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

So far in 2026, Tesla has been struggling to find momentum. The stock has traded under pressure, with its share price down about 9.3% year-to-date. Investors have been focused on lower EV demand, price cuts, and intensifying competition even as Tesla continues to push ahead with its AI and energy ambitions.

In light of that, I see a company whose golden goose is being challenged, but whose profit mix is quietly tilting toward higher-margin, more long-lasting avenues that the market may be undervaluing. I have a neutral stance on TSLA in the near term.
data.author: Tobi Opeyemi Amure

data.title: Oracle's 'Restructuring Is Code for Layoffs,' Analyst Warns — Can ORCL Stock Keep Rising?
data.description:
Shares of Oracle (ORCL) jumped after the company beat fiscal Q3 expectations, with investors cheering the strong results and analysts reaffirming optimism. However, restructuring costs and potential layoffs are drawing attention. RBC Capital analyst Rishi Jaluria highlighted higher restructuring expenses, raising concerns about possible job cuts in the coming months. Looking ahead, analysts rate ORCL as a Strong Buy based on its Q3 performance with an average price target pointing to over 55% upside, though short-term upside may be limited by ongoing restructuring risks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Oracle reported Q3 earnings of $1.79 per share, beating the Street estimate of $1.70, with revenue up 22% year-over-year to $17.19 billion, also surpassing expectations.

Oracle Restructuring Sparks Layoff Fears

According to an SEC (Securities and Exchange Commission) filing on Wednesday, following its Q3 earnings report, Oracle has allocated an extra $500 million for restructuring costs. This brings total FY26 funds to $2.1 billion, far higher than in previous years, suggesting thousands of roles could be cut.

Additionally, the company said in its earnings statement that AI coding tools could let it do more with fewer developers, adding that the technology is "enabling us to build more software in less time with fewer people."

Last week, Bloomberg reported that Oracle is planning to cut thousands of jobs as it manages a cash crunch from its massive AI data center expansion. The planned reductions could affect multiple divisions across the company, reinforcing the link between the increased restructuring fund and the company's broader cost-management strategy.

RBC Flags Risks but Remains Bullish

Jaluria stated that a company doesn't expand its restructuring plan by $500 million for the coming quarter without planning job cuts. He added, "Oracle is signaling that restructuring, which is often code for layoffs, could be broader than earlier this fiscal year."

Nonetheless, Jaluria reiterated his Buy rating on ORCL stock, citing strong growth in results. He noted that sustained share gains will likely depend on disciplined spending, stable margins, and a clear path to free cash flow recovery.

Is ORCL Stock a Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on ORCL stock, based on 28 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average Oracle stock price target of $256.23 implies an upside of 57% from current levels.

Year-to-date, ORCL stock has declined by 16%.
data.author: Kirti Tak
